calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-23-2020 Off Calendar

Petitition For Termination And Discharge
Off Calendar Sep-28-2020 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-22-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Anthony Bentivegna for and with Elia Badra.

Mr. Bentivegna stated that they have overcome the presumption of revocation, that they have a declaration from the estate Attorney explaining that he met with Ms. Frohwein shortly before her death and she affirmed the terms of the pour over will and the trust, and that the Attorney expected that if Ms. Frohwein had changed her mind, she would have contacted him. Mr. Bentivegna also stated that there are no objections, that there is no evidence of revocation, that there is a declaration from Mr. Badra where he was to serve as the Executor, that Ms. Frohwein died unexpectedly, and that there were only a few days between the time Ms. Frohwein met with her Attorney and the time of her death. In addition, Mr. Bentivegna stated that Mr. Badra searched throughout the house, that they interviewed relatives, that they could not find the original will and that bond is waived. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required. Status on close of estate is set for April 14, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
